description: The wing: the limb flight is made of. Built directly on 'wapi' (fly) — anatomy named by what it does, so a learner who knows the verb reads the noun at sight. The bird ('pelori') finally has its instrument.
sound_symbolism: 'wapi' (fly) extended by 'le', the limb-syllable — the verb grown into a body part. The word is its own etymology.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'pelori lo wapile phae.' (The bird opens its wings.) 'wapile lue howeli puro pilu.' (The wing takes strength from the wind.)

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The spread wing is the style's favorite fan of lines — structure and ornament identical, nothing added that does not lift.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Feathered wings taught every tool of air the pre-industrial world had: the fletching, the fan, the sail's cut.
